headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Loses Ground As Iran Deadline Nears; These IBD 50 Names Are Moving (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes continued to show losses Tuesday as President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz approaches.  Meanwhile, Lumentum Holdings showed positive action while UnitedHealth Group topped the Dow on the stock market today.  Stocks remained in the red in the final hour of trading with Dow falling 0.5% and the S&P 500 sliding 0.4%.

headline: Health-Insurance Stocks Rally on Boost to Medicare Payments
summary: Medicare insurers’ shares surged Tuesday morning, as a substantial rate increase from the Trump administration convinced investors that the industry’s political prospects are looking up.  Shares of UnitedHealth, Humana, CVS Health and Elevance Health were sharply higher after the Medicare agency announced a 2027 payment boost of 2.48% on Monday night, a bigger increase than some analysts had expected.  Tuesday’s moves reflect Wall Street’s renewed belief that the Trump administration will be supportive of the private Medicare plans in the future—and investors’ conviction that officials blinked when confronted with traditional industry lobbying tactics, particularly arguments about the dangers of squeezing seniors’ beloved coverage in an election year.

raw.headline: Health-Insurance Stocks Rally on Boost to Medicare Payments
raw.summary: Medicare insurers’ shares surged Tuesday morning, as a substantial rate increase from the Trump administration convinced investors that the industry’s political prospects are looking up.  Shares of UnitedHealth, Humana, CVS Health and Elevance Health were sharply higher after the Medicare agency announced a 2027 payment boost of 2.48% on Monday night, a bigger increase than some analysts had expected.  Tuesday’s moves reflect Wall Street’s renewed belief that the Trump administration will be supportive of the private Medicare plans in the future—and investors’ conviction that officials blinked when confronted with traditional industry lobbying tactics, particularly arguments about the dangers of squeezing seniors’ beloved coverage in an election year.

UnitedHealth is preparing its next earnings report after a year with cyberattack related charges and contract exits. The company is working through a margin recovery plan under a new CEO while addressing cyberattack fallout and unprofitable...
